page: Amazon Web Services f2.12xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor operating at 3.4 GHz. It features 48 dedicated virtual CPUs across 24 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a resource density of 10.67 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided by a 1880 GB NVMe SSD, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 25 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the f2.12xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core CPU performance, scoring in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core tests, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU tests. Its cryptographic capabilities are also strong, supported by hardware-accelerated AES and VAES instruction sets. Memory bandwidth for small, cached blocks is highly efficient, reaching the top 25% tier, though large-block uncached read and write operations fall into the average tier.

The server exhibits average performance for database operations, Redis SET transactions, and static web serving. While it handles small LLM inference text generation efficiently, performance drops significantly for larger models, with 70B parameter text generation falling into the bottom 10%. Given its high memory capacity, dedicated AMD EPYC cores, and fast NVMe storage, this instance is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads, database hosting, and multi-threaded data processing tasks that do not require GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services f2.12xlarge is an x86_64 server powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.4 GHz, featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it includes 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a 1880 GB local NVMe SSD, supported by a 25 Gbps baseline network. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth for cached blocks, placing it in the top 25% for stress-ng and PassMark CPU tests. However, large-block uncached memory transfers and complex LLM inference tasks perform at average or lower tiers. With its high memory-to-core ratio and fast local storage, this instance is optimized for memory-intensive applications, database operations, and multi-threaded data processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services f2.12xlarge is an x86_64 server featuring 48 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 1880 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong multi-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services f2.12xlarge is an x86_64 server with 48 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 1880 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-memory x86_64 server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and fast local NVMe storage for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor at 3.4 GHz
512.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 1880 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Strong multi-core CPU and cryptographic performance in the top 25% of benchmarks
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for memory-intensive databases and multi-threaded data processing
